sowing.method: Planting of nursery-grown seedlings (45-60 days old) in prepared pits

seed_rate.notes: Planting density of 2500-3000 seedlings per hectare under 1.8m x 1.8m layout.

sowing.soil_conditions: Well-drained sandy-loam soils with pH 6.0-6.5. Papaya is extremely sensitive to waterlogging.

irrigation.notes: Irrigate young plants regularly. Use collar drains to prevent collar rot.
Ensure steady moisture. Dry stress triggers flower drop and fruit abortion.
Irrigate consistently. Reduce watering as fruit ripening approaches.

fertilizer.notes: Mix nutrients with farmyard manure and bone meal.

fertilizer.method: Soil surface application in tree basin
fertilizer.notes: Encourages vigorous flowering and fruit set growth.
High potassium boosts fruit sweetness, color, and size.

maturity_indicators: Skin ground color changes from dark green to light green-yellow at apical end (color break)
Latex of fruit becomes watery instead of milky-white
harvest.harvest_method: Hand plucking or using wooden poles with cotton nets
harvest.notes: Harvest fruits carefully to avoid skin scratches. Ripen harvested fruits at room temperature.
